essence: Maintains, repairs, and optimises CNC machines to ensure reliable, accurate, and efficient production.

identity.title: CNC Maintenance Technician
identity.summary: Diagnoses mechanical, electrical, and control-system issues in CNC machines, performs repairs, conducts preventive maintenance, and ensures equipment operates safely and accurately.

salary.notes: Technicians specialising in multi‑axis systems, robotics, or advanced controls can exceed £65k-£80k+.

qualifications: Training in mechanical, electrical, or mechatronic maintenance
Knowledge of CNC machinery, drives, motors, and control systems
Ability to diagnose faults and perform mechanical/electrical repairs
Understanding of preventive maintenance, safety, and calibration

lifestyle.description: CNC maintenance technicians work in precision engineering firms, aerospace, automotive, and industrial manufacturing. They keep CNC machines running reliably, reduce downtime, and ensure production accuracy. The role suits individuals who enjoy diagnostics, hands‑on technical work, and maintaining complex machinery.

habits: Diagnoses mechanical and electrical CNC faults
Performs repairs on motors, drives, spindles, and control systems
Reads schematics, manuals, and engineering documentation
Works with machinists and engineers to minimise downtime
